step_by_step_thinking:
From the references reviewed, particularly articles focusing on dental impression materials like alginate and their setting mechanisms, we understand that warmer water accelerates the setting reaction of alginate. Therefore, raising the temperature of the water used for mixing would help reduce the setting time of the irriversible hydrocolloid.
\boxed{A} Based on the information presented about the impact of water temperature on the setting time of alginate and similar hydrocolloids, increasing the temperature of the water used for mixing will indeed speed up the setting process.
